message:
ui+adrs: wire MCP install in App Store; reconcile ADR-155/156 status

App Store cards now surface the MCP companion-server install line
(cyan-tinted) below the Rust crate install — operators landing on the
substrate page see all three deployment shapes (crate, MCP, npm) at
a glance. Pitch text updated to call out the shipped MCP tool count
("5 MCP tools shipping (1 live, 4 witnessed stubs)" for rvDNA;
"5 MCP tools shipping (3 live: simulate/verify/replay)" for ruQu).

agent-browser e2e check after the rebuild confirmed both new install
lines render exactly as expected:
  $ cargo run -p ruvector-rulake-mcp-rvdna
  $ cargo run -p ruvector-rulake-mcp-ruqu
4 cards present, 0 browser console errors / warnings.

ADR status reconciliation continues from commit 2675aa1:
- ADR-155 (rulake-datalake-layer): bumped M1 → M3 to reflect
  gcs/ipfs storage adapters + rvdna/ruqu substrate adapters + three
  companion MCP servers all shipping. Iceberg/Delta/BigQuery remain
  M4 roadmap.
- ADR-156 (memory-substrate positioning): Proposed → Accepted —
  the framing appears verbatim in the Console, README, and deep
  gists, so the positioning is materially ratified.

ADR-157 (VectorKernel) and ADR-158 (rotation/qvcache) genuinely
remain Proposed — no kernel impls or rotation code has landed since
they were drafted, so their "Proposed" status is accurate.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

files.patch:
@@ -2,9 +2,28 @@
 
 ## Status
 
-**Accepted (M1)** — core abstraction + LocalBackend + FsBackend shipped
-and measured on branch `research/rulake-datalake-analysis`. Backend
-adapters (Parquet, BigQuery, Iceberg, Delta) are M2+.
+**Accepted — M3 as of 2026-04-27.** Beyond the M1 core abstraction +
+LocalBackend + FsBackend, the standalone repo (per ADR-001) now
+ships:
+- **M2 storage adapters**: `gcs-backend/` (Parquet on GCS) and
+  `ipfs-backend/` v0.1 (witness-anchored bundle distribution by
+  CIDv1, with R-IPFS-1 hard-refuse on CID mismatch in commit
+  `56b497b`);
+- **M3 substrate adapters**: `rvdna-backend/` v0.0.1 (genomic
+  hot-tier, ADR-007) and `ruqu-backend/` v0.0.1 (StateVector quantum
+  execution, ADR-008), both with criterion benches + focused
+  security review on file at `docs/research/{benchmarks,security}/`;
+- **Companion MCP servers**: `mcp-server/` v0.10 (general; commit
+  `56b497b` added symmetric mutation-handler audit + R-MCP-1 fix),
+  `mcp-rvdna/` v0.0.1 (5 tools, witness-drift refusal),
+  `mcp-ruqu/` v0.0.1 (5 tools, qubit-cap + circuit-id guards).
+
+Iceberg / Delta / BigQuery adapters remain roadmapped at M4.
+
+**Originally accepted (M1)** — core abstraction + LocalBackend +
+FsBackend shipped and measured on branch
+`research/rulake-datalake-analysis`. Backend adapters (Parquet,
+BigQuery, Iceberg, Delta) were M2+.
 
 ## Date

@@ -2,9 +2,16 @@
 
 ## Status
 
-**Proposed** — positioning addendum, not a replacement. ADR-155 still
-governs what ruLake *is* as a crate. This ADR records what it *also
-happens to be good for* if an agent brain system wants to sit on top.
+**Accepted (positioning ratified, 2026-04-27)** — the memory-substrate
+framing now appears verbatim in the published Console (App Store +
+Stats screens), the README's "Why agents in particular" section, and
+the deep gists at `docs/gists/`. ADR-155 still governs what ruLake
+*is* as a crate; this ADR captures what it *is also good for* and
+that framing has been adopted in user-facing surfaces.
+
+**Originally proposed (2026-04-23)** — positioning addendum, not a
+replacement. This ADR records what it *also happens to be good for*
+if an agent brain system wants to sit on top.
 
 ## Date
